questions.prompt: Before initiating a registrar transfer for a customer, the NS delegation points at the losing registrar's free DNS. What's the right move?
choices: Initiate the transfer and let DNS continue working at the losing registrar
Delete the DNS records first, then transfer
Move DNS hosting to a stable third party (Cloudflare, etc.) first, update NS, wait 48 hours, then transfer
Transfer first, then sort DNS hosting at the new registrar
questions.explanation: The losing registrar may shut off DNS hosting once the domain transfers. If NS still points at them, DNS goes dark the moment the transfer completes. Move DNS to a separate host first, then transfer the registrar safely.

questions.prompt: A domain just registered last week can be transferred to a different registrar immediately.
questions.explanation: ICANN-style policy locks newly-registered domains for 60 days against transfer. Same lock applies to recently-transferred domains and (for many TLDs) recently-changed registrant contacts.
